content: State-of-the-art 2024–2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as iterative curiosity filters, where the agent’s own existing memory (Topic B’s core agent entity) actively prunes and prioritizes which unsampled data points to query next based on how much new information will reshape existing memory schemas, rather than just maximizing prediction uncertainty. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent’s accumulated memory defines its future learning, rather than working as a passive storage for learning outcomes.
